title: TA Planner & Execution Engineer (E&I) - Bukom
description:
Your role

Our major Oil & Energy Downstream Client is urgently looking for Turnaround Planner & Execution Engineer who will be responsible for planning and an executing turnaround activity safely, efficiently and in accordance with applicable premises, work processes, and standards. You will also provide support to the site electrical infrastructure, AI & MM Projects and MOC work planning and implementation. 

Your role includes all aspects of TA phases from strategic planning, preparation and execution which includes shutdown & decon, maintenance & project execution, commissioning & restart, and lookbacks  & closure for learning and future planning.
You will lead electrical expenditure of the Turnaround including projects for various TA complexities. You will typically plans and executes the electrical works for a multi-block event. Each event may involve 18~24 months preparation period leading to a 1-3 month turnaround execution.

Principal Accountabilities

  • Electrical activities related to turnarounds and other shutdowns in a safe and efﬁcient manner, and in accordance with applicable premises, approved work processes, and standards. Familiar with all TA work processes requirements.
  • Act always in accordance with the policy of health, safety, security & environment and plan for TA activities accordingly. Ensure all HSSE requirements, Technical, and Process Safety Standards, and local requirements i.e., Energy Market Authority Act, Electricity Act, and local Code of Practices are considered in the TA Planning process and comply during execution of work.
  • To ensure sites assets operate safely & reliably, without suffering any process safety incident or causing life-changing events to person(s), the staff is accountable to ensure compliance to those Critical Activities assigned for the Job Position.
  • To deliver effective TA and Project planning for all Projects and TA related activities (Maintenance, Operations, and Projects) for electrical works.
  • Completes estimate of Direct Field Labour (DFL) hours, overheads, materials, and equipment for assigned Event for associated works.
  • Gather scope/work packages with detailed information for planning and execution.
  • Gather scope/work packages with detailed information for planning and execution.
  • Lead comprehensive alignment on electrical scope optimization.
  • Data entry/import of detail job plans and estimates into scheduling tool.
  • Support Management of Change (MOC) process for associated TA activities, including the preparation & implementation of logistic and environment plans and necessary temporary facilities, as required.
  • Identiﬁcation, review & escalation of high-risk activities and communicates key issues & risks to the TA Event Manager.
  • Identify and order long-lead items, tools, equipment, materials/parts and secure services/resources required for TA execution. Ensure all relevant technical, safety & quality requirements are clearly incorporated into the deliverables. Create bills of material for assigned work, requisition material and ensure all materials are delivered before feed-out of unit. Work with block lead(s) and TA Schedulers to ensure TA Event scope is fully integrated into block plan within Primavera and being optimized into the TA Event’s integrated master schedule.
  • Field Coordination: Act as ﬁeld coordinator during the execution of an event – coordination with contractors, update progress schedule, responsible for quality assurance aspects and completion status, verify daily Value of Work Done (VOWD), management of TA ﬁles & records, participate in lessons learned & look back process reviewing for improvements in terms of planning & scheduling.
  • During planning stage, the planners will work on Electrical disciplines related planning activities to enhance the dual skills capability, he/she needs to undergo gap assessment according to Job Competency Proﬁle and attend training(s) for gap closure.
  • During execution, the candidate is competent to carry out electrical infrastructure maintenance activities
  • Upload all infrastructure equipment service reports to SharePoint as records.
  • Management of (scope) Change report back on daily schedule and work with Operations, Discipline Engineers and TA Coordinators on changes.
  • Develops, plans and integrates associated addendum/discovery works into Primavera schedule.
  • TA KPIReport back on daily schedule/activities to support monitor and report TA KPI.
  • TA Budget Support cost management initiatives where applicable – forecast cost and workforce requirements, implement agreed TA contracting strategy & contingency cost, validate/challenge invoiced cost.
  • Upkeep and manage TA Event files and records across all TA phases, including post Event system/record updating.
  • Provides constructive critics of TA Event activities after completion; contribute towards Lesson Learnt/After Action Reviews lookback processes.
  • Working with site SME to develop skill sets for equipment unique to the individual site. 
  • Carry out any other related duties as required, including temporary assignments in other sections within the MTAC department on site.


You are what we are looking for if you:

  • Have Degree/Diploma or equivalent in Electrical Engineering
  • Have minimum 8-10 years relevant working experience in electrical construction/maintenance
  • Are an Authorized Electrical Person (AEP) in M site
  • Have completed HV Operation Course by EMA
  • Have LEW qualiﬁcation preferably
  • Have Oil/petrochemical industry safety course for supervisors.
